title: SAP Security
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of SAP Security.
Learn how SAP protects business data.
Identify common SAP Security Transaction Codes.

introduction.title: What is SAP Security?
introduction.content: SAP Security is responsible for protecting business data, controlling user access and ensuring that only authorized users can perform specific activities within the SAP system. It safeguards sensitive business information while maintaining system integrity and compliance.

business_purpose.title: Why is SAP Security Important?
business_purpose.content: Organizations store confidential financial, production, customer and employee information in SAP. SAP Security prevents unauthorized access, protects critical business data and ensures compliance with organizational security policies.

security_components.name: User Master Record
security_components.description: Stores login credentials and user-specific information.

security_components.name: Roles
security_components.description: Defines the activities a user is allowed to perform.

security_components.name: Authorization Objects
security_components.description: Control access to transactions and business data.

security_components.name: Profiles
security_components.description: Collection of authorization settings assigned to users.

integration.purpose: Provides user administration and technical security.

best_practices: Assign users the minimum required access.

business_example.title: Example
business_example.content: A Production Planning executive receives access only to PP transactions, while a Finance executive receives access to FI transactions. This role-based authorization ensures employees can perform only the tasks relevant to their responsibilities.
